Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a client with their associated details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

companyName.description: The name of the client's company.

contactName.description: The name of the primary contact person at the client's company.

contactEmail.description: The email address of the primary contact person.

contactPhone.description: The phone number of the primary contact person.

address.description: The physical address of the client's company.

latitude.description: The latitude coordinate of the client's address.

longitude.description: The longitude coordinate of the client's address.

consumptionData.description: Data related to the client's consumption patterns or history (e.g., JSON string).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Proposal entity.

title.description: The title of the sales proposal.

content.description: The main content or description of the proposal (e.g., JSON string).

createdAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the proposal was created.

updatedAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the proposal was last updated.

SalesMaterial.title: SalesMaterial
SalesMaterial.description: Represents a sales material document or link.

id.description: Unique identifier for the SalesMaterial entity.

title.description: The title of the sales material.

url.description: The URL or path to the sales material.

description.description: A brief description of the sales material.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Commission entity.

userId.description: Reference to User. (Relationship: User 1:N Commission). This represents who is receiving the commission.

createdAt.description: The timestamp indicating when the commission was recorded.

UserProfile.title: User
UserProfile.description: Represents a user of the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

team.description: The sales team the user belongs to.

photoURL.description: URL of the user's profile picture.

onboardingCompleted.description: Flag to indicate if the user has completed the onboarding process.

definition.description: Stores client details.  Includes client's core information.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the client.

definition.description: Stores sales proposals related to a specific client. Includes the proposal details such as title, content, and timestamps.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the client.

params.name: proposalId
params.description: The unique identifier for the proposal.

definition.description: Stores sales material documents and links.  Accessible to sales and admin users.

params.name: salesMaterialId
params.description: The unique identifier for the sales material.

definition.description: Stores sales commission records.  Includes userId to validate ownership via request.auth.uid.

params.name: commissionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the commission record.

definition.description: Stores sales user profiles. Accessible only to the authenticated user (path-based ownership).

params.name: salesId
params.description: The unique identifier for the sales user, matching their Auth UID.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to support the SmartSales AI application's requirements, focusing on security, scalability, and ease of maintenance. The structure adheres to the principles of Authorization Independence, Clarity of Intent, DBAC (Database-Based Access Control), and QAPs (Rules are not Filters). It leverages denormalization to avoid hierarchical authorization dependencies and structural segregation for homogeneous security postures. The structure uses path-based ownership for private data, membership maps for collaborative data (if needed in future iterations), and explicit state modeling.

**Authorization Independence:**

*   The design avoids `get()` calls in security rules by denormalizing authorization data where necessary. For instance, if access to `Proposals` needed to be restricted based on client attributes, those attributes could be copied into the `Proposal` documents. However, given the current information, it is assumed the sales team is able to manage all the proposals, so it is not needed to denormalize data.
*   Commissions are assigned to individual users. Thus, the commissions documents include the `userId` that is validated against `request.auth.uid`.

**Structural Segregation:**

*   The structure maintains a clear separation between user-owned data and shared data. User profiles are stored under `/sales/{salesId}`, ensuring that each user's data is isolated. Other collections are segregated to enforce uniform security requirements.

**Access Modeling:**

*   Private Data: `/sales/{salesId}` stores user-specific information, accessible only to the authenticated user.
*   Hierarchical Paths: `/clients/{clientId}/proposals/{proposalId}` establishes clear ownership and relationship between clients and their proposals.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**

*   The structure allows for secure `list` operations by avoiding the need to filter based on data within documents. Security rules can efficiently validate access based on path and user roles without requiring complex filtering logic.

In summary, this structure prioritizes a simple, secure, and scalable approach by enforcing authorization independence, leveraging structural segregation, and adhering to consistent access modeling patterns.
